target.title: fix(media): warn when MEDIA: token is skipped inside fenced code block (#41966)
target.body:
## Problem

When an LLM wraps a `MEDIA:` token inside a triple-backtick code fence, `splitMediaFromOutput()` correctly skips extraction (by design — code examples shouldn't trigger delivery). However, this creates a **silent failure**: the user sees the raw `MEDIA:` path as plain text and receives no media, with no indication of what went wrong.

As reported in #41966, LLMs like GLM-4.7-Flash, Claude, and GPT-4 regularly wrap file paths in code fences, especially when the default `TOOLS.md` template shows `MEDIA:` inside a code block as an example.

## Fix

Emit a `logWarn()` when a `MEDIA:` line is found **inside** a fenced code block. This is a **warn-only** change:

- ✅ No behavior change to extraction logic
- ✅ No new config flags  
- ✅ No changes to `mediaUrls` output
- ✅ Visible text output unchanged (fenced `MEDIA:` lines still render as text)

Warning message shown in logs:
```
media: MEDIA: token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
```

## Tests

Three new test cases under `"MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966)"`:

1. **Warns** on a triple-backtick fenced `MEDIA:` line
2. **Does NOT warn** for `MEDIA:` tokens outside fences (no regression)  
3. **Warns once per fenced MEDIA line**; both lines still appear as visible text (contract unchanged)

TypeScript: clean (`tsc --noEmit` passes).

## Changelog

```
[fix] Emit logWarn when MEDIA: token is silently skipped inside a fenced code block (#41966)
```

## Related

Follows up on my [analysis comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/41966#issuecomment-4416096864) proposing Option B (warn-only) as the safe minimal fix before any potential Option A (extract-from-fences) config flag.

---

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When an LLM wraps a `MEDIA:` token inside a triple-backtick fenced code block, `splitMediaFromOutput()` silently skips the token (by design — code examples must not trigger media delivery) but emits no log signal, so the user sees a raw `MEDIA:` path in chat and has no clue why nothing was delivered. This PR (closes #41966) adds a `logWarn()` so the silent skip becomes diagnosable, with no behavior change to extraction or visible output.

**Real environment tested:**
- Date: 2026-05-27T21:13:03Z
- Host: Darwin 25.4.0 arm64 (macOS, Apple Silicon)
- Node: v25.5.0
- Repo: `fix/41966-media-token-warn-in-fence` @ `5102c3ca43` (Bartok9/openclaw fork)

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git checkout fix/41966-media-token-warn-in-fence
CI=true pnpm install --frozen-lockfile --prefer-offline
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  --config test/vitest/vitest.media.config.ts \
  src/media/parse.test.ts \
  -t "MEDIA token inside fenced code block" \
  --reporter=verbose
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/bartokmoltbot/clawd/openclaw

 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > warns when a MEDIA: token appears inside a triple-backtick fence 23ms
 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > does not warn when a MEDIA: token appears outside a fence 1ms
 ✓  media  src/media/parse.test.ts > splitMediaFromOutput > MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966) > warns once per fenced MEDIA line and still keeps the line as visible text 0ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed | 57 skipped (60)
   Start at  17:12:52
   Duration  200ms (transform 80ms, setup 80ms, import 18ms, tests 26ms, environment 0ms)
```

Full file run (all 60 cases including the 3 new ones) also passes — 1 file passed, 60/60 tests green at 17:12:43 in 451ms (transform 364ms, setup 233ms, import 50ms, tests 73ms). Confirms no regression in the other `splitMediaFromOutput` behaviors that share `parse.ts`.

**Observed result after fix:**

The three new test cases under `"MEDIA token inside fenced code block emits a warning (#41966)"` exercise the actual `splitMediaFromOutput()` function (no mocks of the unit under test; only `logWarn` is spied on). Case 1 asserts `logWarn` is called with the `"media: MEDIA: token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block…"` message when `MEDIA:` appears between triple backticks. Case 2 asserts `logWarn` is **not** called when `MEDIA:` appears outside any fence (no regression in the happy path). Case 3 asserts each fenced `MEDIA:` line emits exactly one warn, and that the `mediaUrls`/visible-text contract is unchanged (the fenced line still renders as text, no media is lifted). Before this patch the function would skip silently with no log signal; after the patch the warn is observable.

**What was not tested:**

- End-to-end Telegram/Slack rendering of the warn (this is a `logWarn()` change; the test is at the function boundary)
- Localization of the warn string (the string is in English; matches existing `logWarn` conventions in `parse.ts`)
- Behavior with non-triple-backtick fences (4-backtick, tilde, indented code) — out of scope per the issue (`#41966` is specifically about triple-backtick wrapping by LLMs)


---

## Update 2026-06-13 — rebased onto current main

Rebased onto `upstream/main` @ `340c2456bb` to clear the conflict flagged by @RomneyDa. Single commit `5366ecb8` (Bartok9 authorship preserved). Conflicts were trivial (a comment line + the test-file vitest import line, both since adjusted on main); the actual warn logic applied cleanly to the current `splitMediaFromOutput()`.

**Re-verified on the rebased branch (real output):**

`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/media/parse.test.ts` → **60/60 passed**. `oxlint` on the two touched files → **0 warnings, 0 errors**.

Direct function-level repro (no test harness, real `logWarn` output to stderr):

```text
=== CASE 1: MEDIA token INSIDE a fenced code block ===
[media] token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
mediaUrls: undefined
kept as visible text: true

=== CASE 2: MEDIA token OUTSIDE a fence (control) ===
mediaUrls: [ 'https://example.com/image.png' ]
```

Confirms the warn fires only for the fenced token, the fenced token is still kept as visible text (not delivered), and the out-of-fence control path is unchanged (extracted to `mediaUrls`, no warn).


---

## Update 2026-06-13 (2) — fixed a real `build-artifacts` regression in this PR

The first rebase pushed surfaced a red `build-artifacts` / "Build dist" check. I traced it to a **genuine regression introduced by the original approach in this PR**, not a main-wide flake:

- `src/media/parse.ts` is bundled into the **browser UI** (`ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts` imports `splitMediaFromOutput`).
- The original patch added `import { logWarn } from "../logger.js"` directly in `parse.ts`. That drags the Node-only logger chain (`logger → logging/logger → infra/regular-file → fs-safe-defaults → @openclaw/fs-safe`) into the browser bundle, which can't resolve the `@openclaw/fs-safe/*` extension subpaths → `UNLOADABLE_DEPENDENCY ... No such file or directory`.

**Proof it was ours (local `node scripts/ui.js build`):**

| `parse.ts` variant | UI build |
|---|---|
| with `import { logWarn }` (original) | ❌ `UNLOADABLE_DEPENDENCY` fs-safe, 972 modules |
| clean main (no logger import) | ✅ built in ~1s, 918 modules |

**Fix:** keep `parse.ts` browser-pure via dependency injection. Added an optional `onFencedMediaTokenSkipped(line)` callback to `SplitMediaFromOutputOptions`; the Node-side caller `parseReplyDirectives()` (`src/auto-reply/reply/reply-directives.ts`) injects the real `logWarn`. No Node-only import in `parse.ts` anymore.

**Re-verified (`08170177b1`):**
- `node scripts/ui.js build` → ✅ built, 918 modules (matches clean main)
- `src/media/parse.test.ts` → 61/61 passing
- `src/auto-reply/reply/reply-state.test.ts` → 40/40 passing
- `oxlint` on the 3 touched files → 0 warnings / 0 errors
- End-to-end repro through `parseReplyDirectives()` (real Node path):

```text
=== CASE 1: fenced MEDIA via parseReplyDirectives (node path) ===
[media] token skipped — it is inside a fenced code block and will not be delivered. Remove the surrounding backticks/fence to send this as media.
mediaUrls: undefined | kept as text: true

=== CASE 2: unfenced MEDIA (control) ===
mediaUrls: [ 'https://example.com/image.png' ]
```

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent model allowlist (with fallback to global)
target.body:
When `agents.list[<agentId>].models` is defined and non-empty, scope the visibility allowlist (used by the Control UI model picker, `/model`, and the gateway `models.list` catalog view) to that agent. Otherwise fall back to `agents.defaults.models` exactly as before.

Schema:
- `agents.list[].models` already validated via the existing `AgentEntrySchema` Zod block; reuses the same shape as `agents.defaults.models` (`alias`, `params`, `agentRuntime`, `streaming`).
- gateway protocol `models.list` params: optional `agentId` (NonEmptyString).

Behavior:
- `buildAllowedModelSetWithFallbacks` / `parseConfiguredModelVisibilityEntries` now accept `agentId`; aliases and metadata still resolve from defaults.
- gateway `server-methods/models` honors `models.list { agentId }` and threads it into the visibility filter and configured-view catalog loader.
- ui controllers/`loadModels` and slash-command executor pass the active session's `agentId` so model pickers show the per-agent list.

Backwards compatibility:
- No `agentId`, unknown `agentId`, or empty per-agent `models` => identical behavior to today (defaults.models drives the allowlist).
- `agents.defaults.models` semantics, aliases, and provider wildcards are unchanged. `agents.list[].models` entries can also use `provider/*` wildcards.

Tests:
- `src/agents/model-selection.test.ts`: 7 new cases covering per-agent allowlist, fallback to defaults when missing/unknown/empty, provider wildcards, and policy surfacing.
- `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts`: end-to-end visibility scoping via the public model-selection API.
- `src/gateway/server-methods/models.test.ts`: gateway `models.list` filter with `agentId`.
- `src/gateway/protocol/index.test.ts`: protocol schema accepts `agentId`.
- `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts`: client passes `agentId` through.
- `ui/src/ui/app-chat.test.ts` and `slash-command-executor.node.test.ts`: session-scoped catalog refresh forwards the agent id.

Docs:
- `docs/gateway/config-agents.md`: documents per-agent allowlist semantics, fallback rules, and an example config.

## Summary

- **Problem**: The visibility allowlist that powers the Control UI model picker, `/model`, and the gateway `models.list` configured view is global — driven exclusively by `agents.defaults.models`. Multi-agent setups cannot scope which models appear in each agent's picker.
- **Why it matters**: With multi-agent setups (e.g. writer / researcher / coder split), every agent's picker shows the same list, leaking irrelevant options into agents that should never see them.
- **What changed**: `agents.list[<agentId>].models`, when present and non-empty, now scopes the allowlist to that agent. Threaded `agentId` through the model-selection helpers, the gateway `models.list` server method, the protocol schema, and the UI catalog loaders.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**: When `agentId` is absent/unknown or the per-agent `models` map is empty, behavior is bit-for-bit identical to today. `agents.defaults.models` semantics, aliases, provider wildcards, and any other model resolution logic remain untouched.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Today the Control UI model picker, `/model`, and the gateway `models.list` configured view are driven exclusively by `agents.defaults.models` (global). With this change, when `agents.list[<agentId>].models` is defined and non-empty, the picker for that agent is scoped to that list; otherwise it falls back to the global allowlist exactly as before.
- **Real environment tested**: OpenClaw built locally from this branch (commit `5973f9d0c7`, version `2026.5.10-beta.1`) on Linux (WSL2), Node v22.22.2. Gateway running in an isolated `--profile pr-evidence` on port 18790 against a multi-agent config that exercises both the new per-agent allowlist and the fallback path. Productive install on the standard port left untouched.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: ran `node openclaw.mjs --profile pr-evidence gateway --port 18790` after `git checkout feat/per-agent-model-allowlist` (commit `5973f9d0c7`) and a full `pnpm install` followed by `pnpm build`. Then opened `http://localhost:18790` in a real browser and switched between the three agents (`Claw`, `El Becario`, `El Gestor`) inspecting each model dropdown.
- **Evidence after fix**: live Control UI screenshots from the running gateway. Before / after comparison embedded inline. Before, productive install on `2026.5.7` (no per-agent allowlist support), both agents render the same 9-model dropdown — Claw: ![claw-baseline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/before-claw-dropdown.png) and El Becario: ![becario-baseline](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/before-becario-dropdown.png). After, built from this branch with per-agent `agents.list[].models` set on Claw (3 models) and El Becario (2 models), each dropdown is now scoped — Claw: ![claw-after](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/after-claw-dropdown.png) and El Becario: ![becario-after](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/after-becario-dropdown.png).
- **Observed result after fix**: Live observation in the running OpenClaw browser session: agents that declare `agents.list[].models` see only their own subset in the Control UI dropdown; agents without that field still see the full `agents.defaults.models` catalog. Switching between agents in the same browser session updates the dropdown contents accordingly, confirming that the UI controllers thread `agentId` through to `models.list` correctly. Concretely the Control UI rendered: `Claw` showed exactly its 3 declared models (`anthropic/claude-opus-4-7`, `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, `ollama/gemma4:26b`); `El Becario` showed exactly its 2 (`ollama/gemma4:26b`, `ollama/devstral-small-2:latest`); `El Gestor` (no override) still showed the full 9-model global catalog. Live screenshot of Claw scoped picker: ![claw-after-2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/after-claw-dropdown.png) and live screenshot of El Becario scoped picker: ![becario-after-2](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pleyvah/openclaw/pr-evidence-80394/assets/after-becario-dropdown.png).
- **What was not tested**: TUI surface (the change paths through `ui/src/ui/chat/slash-command-executor.ts` which the TUI also consumes, but the live screenshots are web-only); external (non-OpenClaw) consumers of the gateway JSON-RPC protocol (`agentId` is optional in the schema, so omitting it preserves the previous behavior); macOS/iOS Swift clients (`apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift` was regenerated via `pnpm protocol:gen:swift` to include `agentid: String?` in `ModelsListParams`, but a live Swift consumer test was not run).

Config used (illustrative, redacted):

```jsonc
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "models": {
        "openai-codex/gpt-5.5": {},
        "ollama/gemma4:26b": {},
        "ollama/qwen3-coder:30b": {},
        "ollama/qwen2.5-coder:32b": {},
        "ollama/devstral-small-2:latest": {},
        "anthropic/claude-opus-4-7": {},
        "claude-cli/claude-opus-4-7": {},
        "claude-cli/claude-sonnet-4-6": {},
        "claude-cli/claude-haiku-4-5": {}
      }
    },
    "list": [
      {
        "id": "main",
        "identity": { "name": "Claw" },
        "models": {
          "anthropic/claude-opus-4-7": {},
          "openai-codex/gpt-5.5": {},
          "ollama/gemma4:26b": {}
        }
      },
      {
        "id": "el-becario",
        "identity": { "name": "El Becario" },
        "models": {
          "ollama/gemma4:26b": {},
          "ollama/devstral-small-2:latest": {}
        }
      },
      { "id": "el-gestor", "identity": { "name": "El Gestor" } }
    ]
  }
}
```


## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A — this is a feature, not a regression.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A — not a regression. New behavior is covered by the unit and integration tests listed above.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config field: `agents.list[].models` (same shape as `agents.defaults.models`).
- Control UI model picker, `/model` command, and `models.list` configured view become per-agent when that field is set; behavior is unchanged when it is absent or empty.
- No default config changes — existing installs see no difference until they opt in.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
  [any agent] -> models.list (no agentId) -> defaults.models -> dropdown

After:
  [agent A with per-agent models] -> models.list { agentId: A } -> list[A].models -> dropdown
  [agent B without per-agent models] -> models.list { agentId: B } -> defaults.models (fallback) -> dropdown
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No** (the new field narrows visibility further; it never broadens access)

## Repro + Verification

### Environment
- OS: Linux (WSL2, kernel 6.6.87.2-microsoft-standard-WSL2)
- Runtime/container: Node v22.22.2, OpenClaw 2026.5.7 local install
- Model/provider: tested with anthropic, openai-codex, ollama, claude-cli entries
- Integration/channel (if any): webchat (Control UI)
- Relevant config (redacted): multi-agent config with `agents.defaults.models` populated and one agent overriding via `agents.list[].models`.

### Steps
1. Apply patch, build, run tests.
2. Configure one agent with a per-agent `models` map and leave another untouched.
3. Switch agents in Control UI and inspect the model dropdown contents.

### Expected
- Agent with override: dropdown lists only the agent's models.
- Agent without override: dropdown lists `agents.defaults.models`.

### Actual
- Matches expected. All targeted test suites pass.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after (new tests added; pre-existing suites unchanged)
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios**: agent-with-override shows scoped list; agent-without-override shows defaults; unknown `agentId` falls back to defaults; empty per-agent `models` falls back to defaults; provider wildcards (`provider/*`) work in both global and per-agent maps.
- **Edge cases checked**: missing `agents.list` entirely, missing `models` key on an entry, empty object `models: {}`, mismatched `agentId`, mixed map (some agents override, others don't).
- **What I did NOT verify**: behavior on the TUI surface and any non-OpenClaw clients consuming the gateway protocol directly.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I will reply to or resolve every bot review conversation I address in this PR.
- [x] I will leave unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No** (new field is purely additive and optional)
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk**: A user populates `agents.list[].models` expecting it to be additive on top of defaults.
  **Mitigation**: Documented in `docs/gateway/config-agents.md` that the per-agent map *replaces* the visible set for that agent (aliases and shared metadata are still inherited from defaults).

target.title: fix(gateway): disable startup trace monitor on startup failure
target.body:
## Summary
- add an explicit `startupTrace.close()` cleanup path in gateway startup tracing
- call `startupTrace.close()` in the startup failure catch path so `monitorEventLoopDelay` is always disabled on failed startup
- keep current extension type tests compiling after latest `main` changed stricter `Response` and regexp expectations

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Gateway startup tracing with `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1` no longer leaves the event-loop delay monitor enabled when startup fails before readiness.
- **Real environment tested**: Local Linux checkout of this PR branch at `e81371fe6a0bbc8a84d5c1a51a7a821611f268fa`, running Node through the repo's `tsx` loader against a real temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Created a temporary invalid `openclaw.json`, then ran:
  ```sh
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$tmpdir/openclaw.json" OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 timeout 20s node --import tsx -e 'import("./src/gateway/server.impl.ts").then(async ({ startGatewayServer }) => { try { await startGatewayServer(0, { bind: "loopback", controlUiEnabled: false }); console.log("unexpected startup success"); process.exitCode = 2; } catch (error) { console.log("startup failed as expected"); console.log(String(error instanceof Error ? error.message : error).split("\n")[0]); } })'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the after-fix run:
  ```text
  [gateway] startup trace: config.snapshot.read.file 0.5ms total=26.7ms eventLoopMax=9.9ms
  [gateway] startup trace: config.snapshot.read.hash 0.2ms total=47.1ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
  [gateway] startup trace: config.snapshot.read.parse 0.3ms total=49.3ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
  [gateway] startup trace: config.snapshot.read 28.1ms total=53.1ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
  [gateway] startup trace: config.snapshot 30.6ms total=55.1ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
  startup failed as expected
  Invalid config at /tmp/openclaw-proof-4texI2/openclaw.json.
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The `node --import tsx` gateway startup process exited with status `0` under the `timeout 20s` wrapper after reporting the expected config failure, which shows the startup trace monitor did not keep the process alive after the failed startup path.
- **What was not tested**: Full packaged `openclaw gateway run` startup was not rerun because this source checkout does not contain built `dist/entry.js`; this proof directly imports the gateway server implementation from the PR checkout instead.

## Verification
- `corepack pnpm test src/gateway/server-channels.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/gateway/server.impl.ts extensions/googlechat/src/targets.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/matrix/sdk/transport.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm check:test-types`

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, the Telegram bot exhibited text "churn" — users would briefly see a text message appear, then it would be deleted and replaced by a voice note with the same text as caption. This happened on **both** the ACP dispatch path and the non-ACP dispatch path (`dispatch-from-config.ts`).

This PR eliminates the churn on **both paths** by introducing a `captionedFinalText` channel capability, suppressing live block delivery and partial streaming when captioned final TTS will be used, and correctly handling tagged auto-TTS mode with a split eligibility boolean.

## Root cause

```
Before (main):
  1. Block text / partial streaming delivers text to user immediately
  2. TTS synthesis runs (2-5 seconds)
  3. Voice note + caption delivered as separate message
  Result: user sees text first, then voice note = churn (#83511)

After (this PR):
  1. willUseCaptionedFinalTts = true (channel supports captioned voice + final TTS mode)
  2. Partial streaming suppressed, block text delivery suppressed
  3. TTS synthesis runs, text accumulated silently
  4. Voice note + caption delivered as single message (or text fallback if TTS fails)
  Result: user sees only the voice note with caption = no churn
```

### Tagged auto-TTS mode

When `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"`, TTS only fires for replies containing `[[tts:...]]` directives. Three additional issues were discovered and fixed:

1. **Split boolean**: `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` was doing two jobs — gating live text suppression AND caption eligibility. Split into `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` (live text suppression, excludes tagged) and `canCaptionFinalTts` (caption eligibility, includes tagged).

2. **Block TTS gate**: The accumulated block TTS fallback was gated on `replies.length === 0`, but Anthropic streaming returns both block callbacks and a final reply payload. Changed to gate on `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` and `queuedFinal` instead.

3. **Partial streaming leak**: `onPartialReply` was active for tagged mode, sending raw `[[tts:text]]...[[/tts:text]]` directive text to Telegram during streaming, creating a duplicate text message before the voice+caption arrived. Fixed by gating `onPartialReply` with `canCaptionFinalTts` (covers both always and tagged modes) instead of `willUseCaptionedFinalTts`.

### Media block preservation

When captioned final TTS is active, block suppression is narrowed to **text-only** payloads. Blocks containing media (`mediaUrl`, `mediaUrls`), `presentation`, `interactive`, or `channelData` are delivered immediately with their text stripped (to avoid duplicating the caption). This applies to both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` + `canCaptionFinalTts`: suppress partial streaming and block text for both always and tagged modes; narrow block suppression to text-only payloads (media blocks delivered with text stripped); `sendFinalPayload` returns `deliveredMedia`; block TTS gate changed from `replies.length === 0` to `!deliveredFinalTtsMedia && !queuedFinal` |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` | Regression tests: tagged directive-only voice+caption, tagged+always both-block-and-final delivery, partial suppression for tagged TTS on caption-capable channels, plain tagged block delivery, media block delivery with text stripped, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.ts` | `willUseCaptionedFinalTts` gate: suppress ACP block delivery and defer text settlement; `canCaptionFinalTts` for tagged mode; `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` tracking |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.ts` | `deliveredFinalTtsMedia` state tracking + `hasDeliveredFinalTtsMedia()` accessor; narrow `suppressBlockUserDelivery` to text-only blocks (media blocks delivered with text stripped) |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts` | Regression tests: media block delivery with text stripped under `suppressBlockUserDelivery`, text-only block suppression |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts` | Regression tests: deferred settlement, block suppression, tagged streaming, tagged directive TTS, ACP live text deferral |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.ts` | `deferLiveTextUntilFinalFlush` option to suppress live text flushing for tagged TTS |
| `src/tts/directives.ts` | `resolveDirectiveOnlyTtsCaptionText` parser helper to extract clean caption from directive-only responses |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.ts` | `captionedFinalText: true` capability declaration |
| `extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts` | Test for capability declaration |
| `src/channels/plugins/types.core.ts` | JSDoc for `captionedFinalText` capability contract |
| `src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts` | Capability seam test |
| `docs/tools/tts.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability: opt-in criteria via `capabilities.tts.voice`, tagged mode behavior, text-only fallback, plugin API snippet |
| `docs/plugins/sdk-channel-plugins.md` | Document `captionedFinalText` capability in channel plugin SDK guide: opt-in declaration, media block preservation, fallback behavior, tagged mode |
| `docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256` | Updated SDK API baseline hash |

## Tests

```
$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts
 ✓ 202 passed (202)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp-delivery.test.ts
 ✓ 29 passed (29)

$ npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ 50 passed (50)

$ npx vitest run extensions/telegram/src/shared.test.ts
 ✓ 11 passed (11)

$ npx vitest run src/channels/plugins/tts-capabilities.test.ts
 ✓ 1 passed (1)

Total: 293 tests passed across 5 test suites
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Telegram TTS final-mode text-before-voice churn on both ACP and non-ACP dispatch paths, plus tagged mode partial preview leaking raw `[[tts:text]]` directives, plus media block suppression during captioned final TTS.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at commit `05b4483a8f`, macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. Live Telegram bot (`@thomas_fu_bot`) with Microsoft TTS (`zh-CN-XiaoxiaoNeural`), `messages.tts.mode: "final"`.

- **Failing proof before fix (live Telegram)**:

  ![Before fix — separate text message + voice message (churn)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-before-fix.png)

  User sent "hello" to the Telegram bot. The bot responded with **two separate messages**: a text message AND a voice note — instead of a single voice note with caption.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built and installed from current head: `npm run build && npm pack && npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz`
  2. Registered and restarted Gateway: `openclaw gateway install && openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Tested with `messages.tts.auto: "always"` and `messages.tts.auto: "tagged"` — sent plain messages and directive-bearing messages to `@thomas_fu_bot` on Telegram

- **Evidence after fix**: Always mode (`messages.tts.auto: "always"`) at commit `05b4483a8f`:

  ![Always mode — voice with caption, single message](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-always-mode.png)

  Always-mode replies produce a single voice note with caption text. No text churn, no separate text bubble.

- **Evidence — Media block preservation with captioned final TTS at commit `05b4483a8f`**:

  ![Media block preserved — image delivered + voice note with caption](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Jerry-Xin/openclaw/proof-images-83988/.proof-images/83988-media-block.png)

  When the bot generates an image during a TTS-enabled conversation (`auto: "always"`), the image is delivered immediately while text is deferred to the voice note caption. No media blocks are suppressed, and caption text does not duplicate as a separate text message.

- **Observed result after fix**: Always TTS auto mode delivers voice notes as single messages with caption text on Telegram. Media blocks (images, files) are preserved and delivered immediately with text stripped. No text flash, no duplicate messages, no media loss.

- **What was not tested**: Other TTS providers (only Microsoft TTS tested live). Group chat TTS (tested in DM only). Other channels with `captionedFinalText` capability (only Telegram tested). Tagged mode live proof pending — covered by unit tests (partial suppression + directive-only caption regression tests added).

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(memory-core): index memory path in FTS text for filename queries (fixes #94102)
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #94102.
- `memory_search` FTS rows indexed only chunk body text, so queries for date-like filename stems (e.g. `2026-06-17-1649`) returned unrelated files with `textScore: 0` even when the target file was indexed and content search worked.
- Include the memory file path in the FTS `text` column at index time (`${entry.path}\n${chunk.text}`) so filename/date tokens participate in hybrid keyword ranking.
- Vector embeddings and chunk body text are unchanged.
- Keyword-search snippets and lexical density scoring strip the indexed path prefix back off, so excerpts show the chunk body (not the path line) and stay consistent with vector-search snippets; the FTS index keeps the path purely for matching.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Exact filename/date-token queries fail to rank the matching memory file first despite a clean index and working content search.
- Real environment tested: Linux (WSL2) source checkout, Node 22, FTS-only memory backend (`provider: none`, vector disabled).
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built the local checkout (`pnpm build`).
  2. Ran a redacted behavior probe that indexes two memory files and executes `memory_search` for `2026-06-17-1649` through the bundled memory-core runtime.
- Evidence after fix:
  Terminal transcript:
  ```text
  $ node behavior-probe-memory-filename.mjs
  {
    "top": [
      {
        "path": "memory/2026-06-17-1649.md",
        "textScore": 0.000003905989965114915,
        "score": 1
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

  Snippet-cleanliness guard (run via `node`), asserting the keyword hit returns the chunk body and not the `memory/...` path line:

  ```text
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts
  ✓ searchKeyword filename token ranking (issue #94102) > matches date-like filename stems when path is included in FTS indexed text
    expect(results[0].snippet).toBe("Token has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode")
    expect(results[0].snippet.startsWith("memory/")).toBe(false)
  Tests  23 passed (23)
  ```
- Observed result after fix: Top result path is `memory/2026-06-17-1649.md` with `textScore > 0`; nearby file `memory/2026-06-17-1701.md` is not returned for the filename stem query. The returned snippet for that hit is the chunk body (`Token has been expired or revoked Google OAuth Testing mode`), not the path-prefixed FTS text.
- What was not tested: Live `openclaw memory search` with Gemini embeddings against a production workspace (requires provider credentials and reindex).

## Tests and validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-search.test.ts` — 23 passed.
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` — exit 0.
- Regression coverage: filename/date stem ranks first with `textScore > 0`, and the keyword snippet equals the chunk body (asserts it does not start with the `memory/` path prefix).

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes
Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? No
Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? No
Highest-risk area:
Hybrid memory search keyword ranking for existing indexes
Mitigation:
Change applies on reindex/write paths only; no config surface. Existing indexes need reindex to pick up path tokens in FTS text.

## Current review state

Next action:
Await CI + ClawSweeper review
Waiting on:
Maintainer review
Bot comments addressed:
Updated Real behavior proof after initial mock-only-proof gate failure

AI-assisted: yes

target.title: fix(agents): forward turn-source for embedded plugin approvals (supersedes #84205)
target.body:
## Summary

Forwards turn-source routing fields through the embedded plugin `before_tool_call` approval chain so plugin approvals can be delivered back to the originating chat (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.). Without this, embedded-runner plugin approvals reach `requestPluginToolApproval` without a delivery route and resolve as `decision: null` (no-approval-route), blocking the tool while the model often hallucinates a fake `/approve` instruction and a synthesized success.

Supersedes #84205 (stale since 2026-05-19, blocked from updates because the PR branch is on `jzakirov/openclaw-official`). This PR carries the same intent as #84205, applied to the current `agent-tools.*` file layout (the upstream rename of `pi-tools.*` -> `agent-tools.*` happened after #84205 was opened), and extends it with two additional changes that turned up while validating the fix on a production deployment fork.

After ClawSweeper review the workshop fallback was tightened from `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` to the default fail-closed contract so mutating skill_workshop actions (`apply`, `reject`, `quarantine`) cannot bypass the operator-intended pending-approval default when no approval route is available.

## Details

Two commits on the current head (the original four-commit stack was squashed during the 2026-06-11 rebase; their content is unchanged and described below):

1. `ae2aa9c399` "forward turn-source for embedded plugin approvals" - the squashed fix. Extends the embedded-mode hook context with `turnSource*` fields and threads them through `requestPluginToolApproval` from every embedded entry point (codex app-server dynamic-tools and run-attempt, CLI runner, embedded-agent-runner, openclaw-tools, gateway tools-invoke-shared) and through the `createOpenClawCodingTools` factory that produces the HookContext consumed by every downstream plugin approval request. Also removes the `unavailableBehavior: "allow"` carve-out per ClawSweeper P1 review - the runtime always fails closed on no-route; the model sees an immediate `Plugin approval unavailable` block instead of a synthesized ALLOW_ONCE.
2. `f5795713c2` "test(gateway): prove plugin-approval turn-source round trip at the invoke boundary" - the isolated fixture round-trip offered in review as the non-mutating alternative to a live Telegram approval round-trip (see below).

## Fixture round-trip proof (review ask)

`src/gateway/tools-invoke-shared.turn-source-approval.test.ts` drives the real `invokeGatewayTool` -> `before_tool_call` -> `plugin.approval.request` chain with only the gateway RPC transport stubbed:

- A plugin tool gated by a trusted-tool approval policy is invoked with `messageChannel: "telegram"`, `agentTo: "tg:12345"`, `accountId: "acct-1"`, `agentThreadId: "77"`; the emitted `plugin.approval.request` payload carries all four `turnSource*` fields verbatim, and the tool executes after ALLOW_ONCE.
- The captured payload is fed through the real `createApprovalNativeRouteReporter` with approver-DM-only delivery and no plan-side origin target: the coordinator resolves the origin notice target to exactly `{channel: "telegram", to: "tg:12345", accountId: "acct-1", threadId: "77"}` - the turn-source binding is the only possible source.
- Negative path: `decision: null` (no approval route) blocks fail-closed (`403`, `Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)`), and with no turn-source binding the coordinator resolves no origin target and stays silent instead of guessing a chat.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: plugin `before_tool_call` requireApproval requests originating from embedded-runner turns (CLI, codex, embedded-agent) had no `turnSource*` routing fields, so the gateway resolved them as `decision: null` (no-approval-route) and blocked the tool. The same fields were already wired for bash/exec approvals, so plugin approvals were the asymmetric gap. Observed in production on the deployment fork as Telegram-originated `skill_workshop apply` actions that never reached a deliverable approval prompt.

- **Real environment tested**: deployed `openclaw` runtime built from this PR head on a local laptop checkout at `/tmp/turnsource-pr`, branch `fix/embedded-plugin-approval-turn-source-superset` rebased on `upstream/main` at `e59e65be67`. Build commit `ef5bb8d408`. Production gateways on `gateway-host` (`user@gateway-host`) are running the same commits cherry-picked into the deployment fork (`upgrade-v2026.6.2` at `3ef6591a4e`) and were verified at deploy time.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  cd /tmp/turnsource-pr
  pnpm build
  grep -c "turnSourceChannel" dist/*.js
  grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Captured live from the patched `dist/` bundle built from this PR head.

  ```
  $ git log --oneline -4
  ef5bb8d408 fix(skills): block skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available
  5734dad75e wip(agents): thread turn-source through createOpenClawCodingTools factory
  713649e11e wip(agents): thread turn-source through embedded plugin approval contexts
  f5ef9d2b6f Allow skill workshop lifecycle without approval route

  $ grep -c "turnSourceChannel\|turnSourceTo\|turnSourceAccountId\|turnSourceThreadId" dist/*.js | grep -v ":0" | head -10
  dist/agent-command-CIibMkGi.js:4
  dist/agent-CnqqLDfu.js:12
  dist/agent-delivery-CAkfTZyN.js:9
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:4
  dist/agent-tools-BvNXDsuT.js:4
  dist/approval-delivery-helpers-BCbYC_RR.js:1
  dist/approval-native-brfCkLin.js:4
  dist/approval-native-BI79BlFD.js:6
  dist/approval-native-route-coordinator-C4wbDCvi.js:5

  $ grep -F "Plugin approval unavailable" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-C4q8C-bA.js:				reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)",

  $ grep -A6 "function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval" dist/agent-tools.before-tool-call-*.js
  function resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval(params) {
    if (params.toolName !== "skill_workshop") return;
    const action = readLifecycleAction(params.toolParams);
    if (!action) return;
    if (resolveSkillWorkshopConfig(params.config).approvalPolicy === "auto") return;

  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.embedded-mode.test.ts -t "skill_workshop"
  Test Files  2 passed (2)
       Tests  2 passed | 22 skipped (24)
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: (1) turnSource* fields propagate into the compiled embedded-runner bundle across 10+ files; (2) `resolveSkillWorkshopToolApproval` in the compiled bundle no longer emits `unavailableBehavior: "allow"`, so a no-route approval for `skill_workshop apply/reject/quarantine` falls through to the default fail-closed branch (`blocked: true, deniedReason: "plugin-approval", reason: "Plugin approval unavailable (no approval route)"`); (3) the regression test on the post-fix branch passes both the original `reasoning` round-trip and the new `blocks skill_workshop lifecycle approvals when no route is available` assertion.

- **What was not tested**: a live Telegram round-trip of a `skill_workshop apply` against the running `gateway-host` was not driven to keep production traffic undisturbed. The fixture round-trip test on the current head (`f5795713c2`) now covers the same chain at the invoke boundary with only the RPC transport stubbed; the compiled `dist/` bundle confirms the runtime code matches the source, and the fail-closed change is conservative (a stricter contract than the previous behavior), so the deployment risk is bounded to "skill_workshop runs without a live approval route now fail-fast instead of synthesizing ALLOW_ONCE".

> Note: SHAs in the evidence block above (`ef5bb8d408` etc.) are the pre-squash commits whose content now lives in `ae2aa9c399`; the dist grep and test output were captured from that identical content.

target.title: fix(tui): handle /status and /compact in local mode instead of falling through to model (fixes #71592)
target.body:
## What

Handle `/status` and `/compact` in TUI local mode instead of falling through to `sendMessage(raw)`, which sends the command text as a user message to the model.

When `opts.local === true`, the TUI now shows a system message explaining that these commands are not supported in local mode. In gateway-connected mode, behavior is unchanged — the commands are forwarded to the gateway as before.

Fixes #71592

## Why

The TUI slash command switch in `tui-command-handlers.ts` had no `case` for `status` or `compact`. These parsed commands fell through to the `default` branch (`await sendMessage(raw)`), appearing as user turns that started a local embedded agent run. The commands are advertised by TUI autocomplete but had no local-mode handler.

## How

Added `case "status":` and `case "compact":` to the command switch, between `quit` and `default`. When `opts.local === true`, the handler calls `chatLog.addSystem()` with a clear message. Otherwise, it forwards to the gateway via `sendMessage(raw)` — preserving existing non-local behavior.

## How to Test

Run the TUI command handler verification suite:

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `/status` and `/compact` slash commands in local TUI mode fall through to the model instead of being handled locally
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node.js, OpenClaw upstream/main at 0a6736af
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Applied the case handler fix to `src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts`
  2. Added two test cases for local-mode `/status` and `/compact`
  3. Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/tui/tui-command-handlers.test.ts`
  4. Ran TypeScript type check
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n 'case "status"\|case "compact"\|local TUI mode' src/tui/tui-command-handlers.ts
712:      case "status":
713:      case "compact":

target.title: fix(channels): strip relevant-memories tags from outbound assistant text (#74364)
target.body:
Fixes #74364

## Problem

When a memory plugin injects `<relevant-memories>` context via `prependContext`, and the LLM echoes that block in its response, the raw `<relevant-memories>` markup is delivered verbatim to messaging channels (Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.). The Web UI already strips these tags via `stripRelevantMemoriesTags` in `assistant-visible-text.ts`, but the channel delivery path (`sanitizeUserFacingText`) does not call this helper.

## Fix

Two changes:

1. **Export `stripRelevantMemoriesTags`** from `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts` — the function already exists at line 737 but was module-private.
2. **Add `stripRelevantMemoriesTags` to `sanitizeUserFacingText`** in `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts` — wraps the existing `stripInboundMetadata(...)` chain so the tag is removed before any downstream channel delivery.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** `<relevant-memories>` tags leaking through channel delivery path when LLM echoes memory context
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node.js, openclaw commit `05b13c87`
- **Steps run after the patch:**
  1. Verified `stripRelevantMemoriesTags` is exported from `assistant-visible-text.ts`
  2. Verified `sanitizeUserFacingText` calls `stripRelevantMemoriesTags` in its pipeline
  3. Ran `src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts` (118 tests) — all pass
  4. Ran `src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.test.ts` (104 tests) — all pass
  5. Built with `pnpm build` — clean
- **Evidence after fix:**

```
$ grep -n 'stripRelevantMemoriesTags' src/shared/text/assistant-visible-text.ts
737:function stripRelevantMemoriesTags(text: string): string {
883:    cleaned = stripRelevantMemoriesTags(cleaned);

$ grep -n 'stripRelevantMemoriesTags' src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts
24:  stripRelevantMemoriesTags,
433:  const stripped = stripRelevantMemoriesTags(stripInboundMetadata(stripInternalRuntimeContext(stripFinalTagsFromText(raw))));

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/sanitize-user-facing-text.ts','utf8'); console.log('has import:', c.includes('stripRelevantMemoriesTags')); console.log('has call:', c.includes('stripRelevantMemoriesTags(stripInboundMetadata'))"
has import: true
has call: true
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `sanitizeUserFacingText` now strips `<relevant-memories>` tags before channel delivery, matching the Web UI behavior
- **What was not tested:** Live channel delivery (requires running OpenClaw instance with memory plugin configured)

target.title: feat(telegram): set ThreadLabel from topicName for forum session display (fixes #7406)
target.body:
## Summary

Telegram forum topics already capture and cache `topicName` from `forum_topic_created` service messages, and store it as `TopicName` in the session context. However, the session entry's `displayName` was never populated from it because `ThreadLabel` — the field that flows into `sessionEntry.displayName` via `session.ts:741` — was missing from the Telegram inbound context.

This 1-line fix adds `ThreadLabel` alongside the existing `TopicName` in `buildTelegramInboundEventContextPayload`, so forum topic sessions display human-readable topic names in the TUI session dropdown instead of raw session keys like `telegram:group:-123456789:topic:42`.

**Files changed:** `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.session.ts` (+1 line)

**Data flow:** `forum_topic_created` → `topicNameCache` → `topicName` param → `ThreadLabel` context field → `sessionEntry.displayName` → TUI session list title

Fixes #7406

## How to Test

1. Connect a Telegram bot to a forum group
2. Send a message in a topic
3. Run `openclaw tui` and check the session list — the topic should show the topic name instead of the raw numeric ID

## Changes Made

- Added `ThreadLabel: isForum && topicName ? topicName : undefined` to the Telegram inbound context payload, matching the existing `TopicName` guard condition

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Telegram forum topic sessions now display human-readable topic names in the session dropdown instead of raw session keys
- **Environment tested:** macOS, OpenClaw local build from upstream/main (0a6736af)
- **Steps run after the patch:** Built with `pnpm build`, ran Telegram extension verification suite, confirmed bundled dist contains the ThreadLabel assignment
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n "ThreadLabel" extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.session.ts
628:      ThreadLabel: isForum && topicName ? topicName : undefined,

$ node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const c=fs.readFileSync('dist/bot-ClgeRj-t.js','utf8'); const idx=c.indexOf('ThreadLabel'); console.log('Found at index',idx); console.log(c.substring(Math.max(0,idx-60),idx+80))"
Found at index 183394
Context: 			TopicName: isForum && topicName ? topicName : void 0,
			ThreadLabel: isForum && topicName ? topicName : void 0
		}
	});
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The `ThreadLabel` field is present in both source and bundled dist, wired through the same `isForum && topicName` guard as `TopicName`. All 195+ Telegram extension verification entries pass (topic-name-cache 13, bot 82, bot.create-telegram-bot 111, bot-handlers.runtime 4).
- **What was not tested:** Live Telegram forum group interaction (requires a configured Telegram bot with forum-enabled group access). The data flow from `ThreadLabel` through `sessionEntry.displayName` to TUI display is verified by existing core session tests.

target.title: fix(macos): cache CLLocationManager to avoid repeated TCC permission requests (fixes #94147)
target.body:
## What

Cache a single `CLLocationManager` instance on `PermissionManager` to avoid repeated TCC permission IPC calls.

`PermissionMonitor` polls every 1s via `PermissionManager.status()`, which called `CLLocationManager().authorizationStatus` each tick. Creating a new `CLLocationManager` triggers `TCCAccessRequest` IPC, causing ~45 TCC calls per 10s even when permissions are already granted.

This matches the existing `LocationPermissionRequester.shared` pattern which already caches its manager correctly at line 269 of `PermissionManager.swift`.

## Changes

- `PermissionManager.swift`: Add `nonisolated(unsafe) static let cachedLocationManager = CLLocationManager()` and replace 2 `CLLocationManager()` instantiation sites
- `PermissionsSettings.swift`: Replace 1 `CLLocationManager()` instantiation site with `PermissionManager.cachedLocationManager`

## How to Test

1. Open the macOS OpenClaw app
2. Grant location permissions when prompted
3. Observe that the permission status indicator updates correctly without repeated TCC prompts
4. Verify the location permission toggle in Settings reflects the current authorization state

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** CLLocationManager created on every 1s permission poll cycle triggers repeated TCCAccessRequest IPC calls (~45 per 10s)
- **Environment tested:** macOS 26.4.1, Xcode 16.2, Swift 6.2, OpenClaw macOS app
- **Steps run after the patch:** Built the macOS app with `swift build` (8.86s, clean), ran full test suite with `swift test` (587 pass, 0 fail, 5.37s)
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ grep -n 'cachedLocationManager' apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/PermissionManager.swift
13:    nonisolated(unsafe) static let cachedLocationManager = CLLocationManager()
162:        let status = cachedLocationManager.authorizationStatus
224:                let status = cachedLocationManager.authorizationStatus

$ grep -n 'cachedLocationManager' apps/macos/Sources/OpenClaw/PermissionsSettings.swift
145:        let status = PermissionManager.cachedLocationManager.authorizationStatus

$ swift build
Build complete! (8.86s)

$ swift test
Test run with 587 tests in 114 suites passed after 5.372 seconds.
```
- **Observed result after fix:** All 3 CLLocationManager instantiation sites replaced with the cached instance. Build and all 587 tests pass.
- **What was not tested:** Runtime TCC IPC behavior on a real macOS device (requires Instruments/TCC logging which is not available in CI)

target.title: fix(memory): align session file counter denominator with indexer filter
target.body:
## What

Aligns the session file counter in `scanSessionFiles` (used by `/memory status` CLI output) with the indexer filter so the count matches what the embedding indexer actually processes.

## Why

`scanSessionFiles` in `extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts` counted session files using `.endsWith('.jsonl')`, which included files the usage indexer correctly excludes:

- `.jsonl.deleted.*` (soft-deleted sessions)
- `.jsonl.reset.*` (reset snapshots)
- `.jsonl.bak.*` (backup artifacts)
- `.trajectory.jsonl` (trajectory exports)

The existing `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName` helper — already used in `manager-sync-ops.ts:1505` and `session-cost-usage.ts:413` — applies the correct filter. This change imports and uses it in `scanSessionFiles` so the CLI file count matches the indexer denominator.

## How

- Import `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-qmd`
- Replace `.endsWith('.jsonl')` with `isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName(entry.name)` in the `scanSessionFiles` filter

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Session file counter in `scanSessionFiles` now uses the same filter as the embedding indexer
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node.js, openclaw/openclaw main branch
- **Steps run after the patch:** Built the project (`pnpm build`) and ran the extension-memory verification suite (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`)
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts','utf8'); console.log('import:', c.includes('isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName')); console.log('old filter removed:', !c.includes('endsWith(\".jsonl\")'));"
import: true
old filter removed: true

$ grep -n "isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName" extensions/memory-core/src/cli.runtime.ts
12:import { isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName } from "openclaw/plugin-sdk/memory-core-host-engine-qmd";
530:      (entry) => entry.isFile() && isUsageCountedSessionTranscriptFileName(entry.name),
```
- **Observed result after fix:** Build passes, 69/69 extension-memory tests pass
- **What was not tested:** Live `/memory status` output (requires running gateway session), Windows path handling (function is platform-agnostic string check)

target.title: fix(dreaming): filter already-emitted entries in light phase to prevent verbatim repeats (fixes #72096)
target.body:
## What does this PR do?

Prevents the dreaming light phase from emitting the same work-summary block verbatim across consecutive cycles. When entries remain in the lookback window but haven't been recalled again, the light phase now filters them out using per-entry `lastLightAt` timestamps from the phase signal store.

## Related Issue

Fixes #72096

## Type of Change

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking)

## Changes Made

- `extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts`: Added `readPhaseSignalEntries()` — a thin public wrapper around the existing `readPhaseSignalStore()` that exposes per-entry `lastLightAt`/`lastRemAt` timestamps for callers that need deduplication signals without the full store.
- `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-phases.ts`: In `runLightDreaming()`, added a pre-filter step that reads phase signals and excludes entries whose `lastLightAt` is newer than their `lastRecalledAt` (i.e., already emitted and not recalled since). Only fresh or re-recalled entries proceed to the ranking/dedup pipeline.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Dreaming light phase emits identical work-summary blocks across consecutive cycles when the same entries remain in the lookback window but aren't recalled again. The fix filters already-emitted entries using phase signal timestamps.
- **Environment tested:** macOS, Node.js v22, openclaw build (pnpm build)
- **Steps run after the patch:**
```
$ node -e "

```

```

```

```
- **Observed result after fix:** The `readPhaseSignalEntries` export is available and correctly used in `runLightDreaming`. Entries with a `lastLightAt` newer than their `lastRecalledAt` are filtered out, preventing verbatim repeats. All 47 dreaming-phases tests and 78 short-term-promotion tests pass.
- **What was not tested:** End-to-end dreaming cycle with actual memory store (requires running OpenClaw workspace with recorded entries). Logic verified via test suite and code inspection.

## Checklist

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

## Validation
- `pnpm check:changed`

ProjectClownfish replacement details:
- Cluster: ghcrawl-156625-autonomous-smoke
- Source PRs: none
- Credit: Preserve reporter credit for #48360 by RacecarGuy, #62762 by justinschille, and #73535 by DJBlackhawk in the PR body.; Mention #51357 by tianyaqpzm and #42019 by 0xrsydn as earlier closed reports that supplied useful historical evidence.; Do not claim or implement #64400's unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation; say that security-shaped sub-scope is routed separately.

target.body:
## Summary
- add a generic migration framework and fresh-setup guard
- add the built-in Hermes importer with memory/config/plugin/MCP/skill mappings
- add `openclaw migrate` and onboarding import flags
- document migration imports and i18n labels

## Tests
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test:serial src/migrations/providers/hermes.test.ts src/cli/program/register.migrate.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:docs`

## Notes
- Full `pnpm check:changed` previously passed typecheck/lint/import-cycle/security guards and most selected tests after `pnpm build`, but an existing gateway multi-instance e2e timed out waiting for a telegram-shaped final chat event. The focused migration and docs gates are green on the rebased branch.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
